description:
Recto: Bottom of a Judaeo-Arabic letter to a more important person, begging him to do some sort of favor ("wa-yubayyiḍ al-mawlā wajh al-shafāʿa fī dhālik") and not to make the writer come in person. The handwriting seems quite familiar (Eliyyahu the Judge?), likely ca.1200.

Verso: bottom of a Judaeo-Arabic letter in a different hand, resembling that of Avraham Maimonides.
